data.title: Trump Criticizes EU's Fines on U.S. Tech Companies as Unfair
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. President Donald Trump expressed his discontent on Truth Social regarding the substantial fines imposed by the European Union on American technology companies. He described these actions as discriminatory against the exceptional technology and tax practices of the United States. Data indicates that in 2024, the EU's fines on U.S. tech firms amounted to 3.8 billion euros, surpassing the total income tax paid by all publicly listed European internet technology companies, which was 3.2 billion euros during the same period. Major American tech companies such as Apple, Google, and Meta are currently facing billions of euros in fines or tax adjustments from the EU.

data.title: Kaito: Ceasing operations of Yaps and launching Kaito Studio; KAITO tokens will continue to play a role in Kaito Studio.
data.description: Kaito announced on its X platform that it is discontinuing Yaps and its incentivized leaderboards to usher in a new era for Kaito Studio. Kaito Studio will transform into a traditional tiered marketing platform where brands can selectively collaborate with creators based on established criteria. The platform will cover multiple social media channels, including X, YouTube, and TikTok, and its business scope will expand from cryptocurrency to areas such as finance and AI. Kaito stated that this change will not affect Kaito Pro, Kaito API, Kaito Launchpad, or Kaito Markets. The KAITO token will continue to function within Kaito Studio.

data.title: Crypto Holders Lost $17,000,000,000 to Fraudsters in 2025 As Impersonation Scams Explode: Chainalysis
data.description:
Cryptocurrency holders lost an estimated $17 billion to scams and fraud in 2025 as impersonation schemes and AI-enabled attacks explode, according to a new report. Researchers at Chainalysis say on-chain inflows to scam addresses hit at least $14 billion and could exceed $17 billion as more illicit wallets are identified.
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2026/01/15/crypto-holders-lost-17000000000-to-fraudsters-in-2025-as-impersonation-scams-explode-chainalysis/

data.title: Trump: The EU's huge fines against US tech companies are extremely unfair
data.description: US President Trump posted on Truth Social, stating that the EU's massive fines against US tech companies are extremely unfair and discriminatory against American technological excellence and taxation. According to related charts and data, in 2024, the EU's total fines against US tech companies reached €3.8 billion, while the total income tax of all listed European internet tech companies in the same period was only €3.2 billion. Currently, US tech giants such as Apple, Google, and Meta are facing billions of euros in fines or back taxes from the EU.

data.title: DeadLock Ransomware Utilizes Polygon Smart Contracts for Evasion
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, the ransomware family DeadLock is employing Polygon smart contracts to distribute and rotate proxy server addresses, aiming to evade security detection. Initially discovered in July 2025, this malware embeds JavaScript code within HTML files to interact with the Polygon network, using RPC lists as gateways to obtain server addresses controlled by attackers. This technique resembles the previously identified EtherHiding method, which leverages decentralized ledgers to create hard-to-block covert communication channels. DeadLock has released at least three variants, with the latest version incorporating the encrypted communication application Session to directly communicate with victims.
